title: abies lasiocarpa var. arizonica
intro: Abies lasiocarpa var. arizonica, also known as Arizona fir, is a variety of the subalpine fir tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds. The herbal preparations of Abies lasiocarpa var. arizonica include essential oils, teas, and infusions, which are typically used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
